ogTitle: McKinnon vs McLoughlins Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McKinnon and McLoughlins Beach.
heading: McKinnon vs McLoughlins Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McKinnon, Victoria and McLoughlins Beach, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McLoughlins Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to McKinnon (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McKinnon or McLoughlins Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McKinnon has a flood risk score of 59/100, while McLoughlins Beach scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McKinnon scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. McLoughlins Beach scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McKinnon or McLoughlins Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McLoughlins Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
